title: Paraphrase evasion of keyword guard
description: Tests whether PUB-008's overclaim can be rephrased with synonyms so that v0.2's keyword-based negative_space guard fails to flag the contradiction. Opens with the profile's own declared weakness per INV-6.

target.relationship: signal.digest may assert claims that negative_space[4] declares as out of scope

oracle_origin.author: dispatch
oracle_origin.context_exposure: full — author of both artifact and fixture
oracle_origin.prompt_input_hash: PENDING — will be computed from canonicalized input on first run
oracle_origin.fixture_hash: PENDING — will be sha256 of this file with fixture_hash field excluded

runner.note: Each test case requires a patched copy of PUB-008 with the specified modification. Runner outputs JSON with { exit_code, warnings[], errors[], matched_rules[] }.

test_cases.description: Original overclaim — baseline, exact keyword match
test_cases.patch: No modification. PUB-008 signal.digest contains 'structural model'; negative_space[4] references 'structural model'.
test_cases.notes: Validator should flag exact keyword match between signal.digest and negative_space.

test_cases.description: Synonym substitution — 'architectural framework' replaces 'structural model' in signal.digest
test_cases.patch: In signal.digest, replace 'structural model' with 'architectural framework'. negative_space[4] still says 'structural model'.
test_cases.expected_matched_rule: none (keyword guard has no semantic layer)

actual_result_petrovich.note: WARNING triggered but by irrelevant word 'fiedler' matching a different rule, NOT by detecting the synonym. This is a false positive — the guard appears to catch the evasion but actually catches something unrelated. Worse than clean miss because it masks the gap.

test_cases.notes: Petrovich's direct run shows this is a false positive, not a real detection. The guard matched 'fiedler' (a proper noun from spectral graph theory) against an unrelated rule. Profile verdict: FAIL — synonym evasion succeeded, false positive conceals the failure.

test_cases.description: Pure paraphrase — entirely different vocabulary for same claim
test_cases.patch: In signal.digest, replace 'structural model' with 'we identified recurring motifs in information architectures'. negative_space[4] still says 'structural model'.
test_cases.notes: Pure paraphrase with zero lexical overlap. Only semantic analysis could catch this. Current v0.2 has no semantic layer. Expected and confirmed failure.

result_ledger.notes: B fails differently than expected — false positive masks real gap. C fails cleanly as predicted.

interpretation.if_all_pass: Keyword guard has semantic capability not documented in v0.2 spec — requires revision of this fixture's assumptions AND the profile's scope_limitation node
interpretation.if_B_fails_C_fails: Expected behavior. Guard catches keywords, not semantics. PUB-012 scope_limitation is honest. Petrovich's finding that B produces a false positive is worse than clean miss — it creates a confabulation bridge at the validator level.
interpretation.if_all_fail: Guard is weaker than assumed. PUB-012's scope_limitation is more binding than the profile's text admits.

meta.changelog: v0.2: fixed target path (was topology.nodes[structural-model], corrected to signal.digest vs topology.negative_space[4]). Added runner command. Added actual_result_petrovich for B and C. Added result_ledger. Fixed oracle_provenance field names per normative_core. Noted B false positive per Petrovich's direct run.
